senses.definition: used with the numbers 1 to 12 when telling the time, to mean an exact hour
examples: He left between five and six o'clock.
at/after/before eleven o’clock

senses.definition: the quality or fact of being very fat, in a way that is not healthy
examples: Obesity can increase the risk of heart disease.

senses.definition: to do what you are told or expected to do
examples: to obey a command/an order/rules/the law

He has turned into an efficient soldier, blindly obeying orders (= without questioning them).

senses.definition: a thing that can be seen and touched, but is not alive
examples: everyday objects such as cups and saucers
inanimate objects (= things that are not alive)
a physical/material object (= that you can see and touch)

art objects from the ancient world

senses.definition: a person or thing that somebody desires, studies, pays attention to, etc.
examples: He felt he was nothing more to her than an object of desire.

an object of affection/ridicule/worship

senses.definition: an aim or a purpose
examples: Her sole object in life is to become a travel writer.

senses.definition: a noun, noun phrase or pronoun that refers to a person or thing that is affected by the action of the verb (called the direct object), or that the action is done to or for (called the indirect object)
money is not something that needs to be considered, because there is plenty of it available
examples: She travels around the world as if money is no object.

senses.definition: to say that you disagree with or oppose something
examples: If nobody objects, we'll postpone the meeting till next week.

senses.definition: to give something as a reason for opposing something
examples: He objected that the police had arrested him without sufficient evidence.

senses.definition: a reason why you do not like or are opposed to something; a statement about this
examples: I'd like to come too, if you have no objection.

There was widespread objection to the proposals.
I have no objection to him coming to stay.
I have no objection to staying here.

senses.definition: not influenced by personal feelings or opinions; considering only facts
examples: an objective assessment of the situation
objective truth/facts/reality
objective criteria/measures/measurements

It's hard to remain completely objective.

senses.definition: existing outside the mind; based on facts that can be proved
examples: objective reality

senses.definition: the objective case is the one which is used for the object of a sentence
something that you are trying to achieve
examples: the primary/principal/key objective

How does the project fit with our strategic objectives?
the government's policy objectives
to achieve/meet/accomplish your objectives
to set/state/define your objectives

senses.definition: the lens in a telescope or microscope that is nearest to the object being looked at
the state of being forced to do something because it is your duty, or because of a law, etc.
examples: You are under no obligation to buy anything.

senses.definition: something which you must do because you have promised, because of a law, etc.
examples: legal/professional/financial obligations

senses.definition: to force somebody to do something, by law, because it is a duty, etc.
examples: Parents are obliged by law to send their children to school.

senses.definition: to help somebody by doing what they ask or what you know they want
examples: Call me if you need any help—I'd be happy to oblige.

senses.definition: the act of watching somebody/something carefully for a period of time, especially to learn something
examples: Most information was collected by direct observation of the animals' behaviour.
results based on scientific observations

an observation post/tower (= a place from where somebody, especially an enemy, can be watched)
an observation deck (= a room or platform at the top of a tall building which provides views of the surrounding area)
his close observations of animal life in its natural setting

senses.definition: a comment, especially based on something you have seen, heard or read
examples: May I make a personal observation?

her witty observations on life

senses.definition: to see or notice somebody/something
examples: Have you observed any changes lately?

senses.definition: to watch somebody/something carefully, especially to learn more about them
examples: He observes keenly, but says little.
I felt he was observing everything I did.

an observed decrease in rainfall in the area
The program provided an opportunity to observe the effects of treatment.
The cells were observed under a microscope.
The patient should be observed for signs of an allergic reaction.
They observed how the parts of the machine fitted together.

senses.definition: to make a remark
examples: She observed that it was getting late.

senses.definition: to obey rules, laws, etc.
examples: Will the rebels observe the ceasefire?
The crowd observed a minute's silence (= were silent for one minute) in memory of those who had died.

senses.definition: to celebrate festivals, birthdays, etc.
examples: Religious festivals were always observed.

senses.definition: a person who watches somebody/something
examples: According to observers, the plane exploded shortly after take-off.

senses.definition: a person who attends a meeting, lesson, etc. to listen and watch but not to take part
examples: A team of British officials were sent as observers to the conference.

senses.definition: a person who watches and studies particular events, situations, etc. and is therefore considered to be an expert on them
examples: a royal observer

senses.definition: to completely fill your mind so that you cannot think of anything else, in a way that is not reasonable or normal
examples: He's obsessed by computers.

senses.definition: to be always talking or worrying about a particular thing, especially when this annoys other people
examples: I think you should try to stop obsessing about food.

senses.definition: the state in which a person’s mind is completely filled with thoughts of one particular thing or person in a way that is not reasonable or normal
examples: Her fear of flying is bordering on obsession.

senses.definition: a person or thing that somebody thinks about too much
examples: Fitness has become an obsession with him.

senses.definition: a situation, an event, etc. that makes it difficult for you to do or achieve something
examples: So far, we have managed to overcome all the obstacles that have been placed in our path.

senses.definition: an object that is in your way and that makes it difficult for you to move forward
examples: The area was full of streams and bogs and other natural obstacles.

senses.definition: a fence, etc. for a horse to jump over
examples: This huge open ditch forms the biggest obstacle on the course.

senses.definition: to get something, especially by making an effort
examples: to obtain information/data/results

Further details can be obtained by writing to the above address.

senses.definition: to exist
examples: These conditions no longer obtain.

senses.definition: easy to see or understand
examples: I know you don't like her but try not to make it so obvious.

senses.definition: that most people would think of or agree to
examples: She was the obvious choice for the job.

senses.definition: not interesting, new or showing imagination; unnecessary because it is clear to everyone
examples: The ending was pretty obvious.

senses.definition: used when giving information that you expect other people to know already or agree with

senses.definition: used to say that a particular situation or fact is easy to see or understand
examples: He was obviously upset.
They're obviously not coming.
‘I didn't realise it was a formal occasion.’ ‘Obviously!’ (= I can see by the way you are dressed)

senses.definition: a particular time when something happens
examples: on this/that occasion

senses.definition: a special event, ceremony or celebration
examples: a great/memorable/happy/momentous occasion

senses.definition: a suitable time for something

senses.definition: a reason or cause

I'm willing to go to court over this if the occasion arises (= if it becomes necessary).

senses.definition: sometimes but not often
examples: He has been known on occasion to lose his temper.

senses.definition: a feeling or understanding that an event is important or special
examples: Candles on the table gave the evening a sense of occasion.

senses.definition: happening or done sometimes but not often
examples: He works for us on an occasional basis.

senses.definition: sometimes but not often
examples: We occasionally meet for a drink after work.

senses.definition: a job or profession

senses.definition: the way in which you spend your time, especially when you are not working
examples: Her main occupation seems to be shopping.

senses.definition: the act of moving into a country, town, etc. and taking control of it using military force; the period of time during which a country, town, etc. is controlled in this way

senses.definition: the act of living in or using a building, room, piece of land, etc.

senses.definition: to fill or use a space, an area or an amount of time

senses.definition: to live or work in a room, house or building

senses.definition: to enter a place in a large group and take control of it, especially by military force
examples: The capital has been occupied by the rebel army.

senses.definition: to fill your time or keep you busy doing something
examples: a game that will occupy the kids for hours

senses.definition: to have an official job or position
examples: The president occupies the position for four years.

senses.definition: to happen
examples: When exactly did the incident occur?

senses.definition: to exist or be found somewhere
examples: Sugar occurs naturally in fruit.

senses.definition: something that happens or exists
examples: a common/everyday/frequent/regular occurrence

senses.definition: the fact of something happening or existing

senses.definition: the mass of salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface

ships large enough to sail the open ocean
an ocean liner
a species of fish that lives on the ocean floor

senses.definition: one of the five large areas that the ocean is divided into

senses.definition: an amount of something that is too small or unimportant to make any real difference to a situation
examples: The amount of money raised was a drop in the ocean compared to what we needed.

senses.definition: a large amount of something
the 10th month of the year, between September and November
examples: She was born in October.

The meeting is on October fifth.

last October's election
the October issue of the magazine

senses.definition: strange or unusual
examples: They're very odd people.

a teenage girl with rather odd hair

senses.definition: strange or unusual in the way mentioned

senses.definition: that cannot be divided exactly by the number two

senses.definition: happening or appearing occasionally; not happening often

senses.definition: of no particular type or size; various

senses.definition: not with the pair or set that it belongs to; not matching
examples: You're wearing odd socks!

senses.definition: available; that somebody can use

senses.definition: approximately or a little more than the number mentioned
examples: How old is she—seventy odd?

senses.definition: a person or thing that is different from others or does not fit easily into a group or set

senses.definition: a person who is slightly strange or crazy

senses.definition: to be different from something, when the two things should be the same

senses.definition: you are unlikely to succeed because the conditions are not good for you

examples: It makes no odds to me whether you go or stay.

senses.definition: more money than you would normally expect

senses.definition: belonging to somebody; relating to somebody

the love of a mother for her child
the role of the teacher

the paintings of Monet

senses.definition: belonging to something; being part of something; relating to something
examples: the lid of the box
the director of the company
a member of the team
the result of the debate

senses.definition: coming from a particular background or living in a place
examples: a woman of Italian descent
the people of Wales

senses.definition: relating to or showing somebody/something
examples: a story of passion
a photo of my dog
a map of India

senses.definition: used to say what somebody/something is, consists of or contains
examples: the city of Dublin
the issue of housing
a crowd of people
a glass of milk

senses.definition: used with measurements and expressions of time, age, etc.

a girl of 12
the fourth of July
the year of his birth

senses.definition: used to show somebody/something belongs to a group, often after some, a few, etc.

senses.definition: used to show the position of something/somebody in space or time

senses.definition: used after nouns formed from verbs. The noun after ‘of’ can be either the object or the subject of the action.

the howling of the wind

senses.definition: used after some verbs before mentioning somebody/something involved in the action

senses.definition: used after some adjectives before mentioning somebody/something that a feeling relates to

senses.definition: used to give your opinion of somebody’s behaviour

senses.definition: used when one noun describes a second one

senses.definition: used before a noun to say that something is very surprising
examples: I'm surprised that you of all people should say that.

senses.definition: used to express anger

senses.definition: used to say that something has been removed or become separated
examples: He's had his beard shaved off.

senses.definition: not connected or functioning

senses.definition: down or away from a place; at a distance in space or time

senses.definition: away from work or duty

senses.definition: starting a race

senses.definition: taken from the price

senses.definition: no longer going to happen; cancelled

senses.definition: no longer available or being served

senses.definition: behind or at the sides of the stage in a theatre
to be in a better or worse situation
examples: She's better off without him.

senses.definition: to have a particular amount of something
examples: How are we off for coffee (= how much do we have)?

senses.definition: used to say how much money somebody has

senses.definition: from time to time; now and again

senses.definition: down or away from a place; at a distance in space or time
examples: I fell off the ladder.

senses.definition: used to say that something has been removed
examples: You need to take the top off the bottle first!

senses.definition: away from work or duty
examples: He's had ten days off school.

senses.definition: away from a price

senses.definition: leading away from something, for example a road or room
examples: We live off Main Street.

senses.definition: from a person or place

senses.definition: off; from

senses.definition: an illegal act

a capital offence (= one for which somebody may be punished by death)

senses.definition: the feeling of being upset or angry at something that somebody has said or done

senses.definition: used to say that you do not mean to upset or show a lack of respect for somebody by something you say or do

senses.definition: a person who commits a crime

senses.definition: connected with the team that has control of the ball; connected with the act of scoring points

senses.definition: an amount of money that somebody is willing to pay for something

senses.definition: used to say that it is somebody’s turn to do something
resting on the surface of somebody/something and partly or completely covering them/it
examples: I put a blanket over the sleeping child.
He wore an overcoat over his suit.
She put her hand over her mouth to stop herself from screaming.

senses.definition: in or to a position higher than but not touching somebody/something; above somebody/something
examples: They held a large umbrella over her.
The balcony juts out over the street.
There was a lamp hanging over the table.

senses.definition: from one side of something to the other; across something
examples: a bridge over the river
They ran over the grass.
They had a wonderful view over the park.

senses.definition: so as to cross something and be on the other side
examples: She climbed over the wall.

senses.definition: falling from or down from a place
examples: The car had toppled over the cliff.
He didn't dare look over the edge.

senses.definition: on the far or opposite side of something
examples: He lives over the road.

senses.definition: in or on all or most parts of something
examples: Snow is falling all over the country.
They've travelled all over the world.
There were papers lying around all over the place.

senses.definition: more than a particular time, amount, cost, etc.
examples: over 3 million copies sold
She stayed in Lagos for over a month.
He's over sixty.

senses.definition: used to show that somebody has control or authority
examples: She has only the director over her.
He ruled over a great empire.
She has editorial control over what is included.

senses.definition: during something
examples: We'll discuss it over lunch.
Over the next few days they got to know the town well.
She has not changed much over the years.
He built up the business over a period of ten years.
We're away over (= until after) the New Year.

senses.definition: past a particular difficult stage or situation
examples: We're over the worst of the recession.
It took her ages to get over her illness.

senses.definition: because of or relating to something; about something
examples: an argument over money
a disagreement over the best way to proceed

senses.definition: using something; by means of something
examples: We heard it over the radio.
She wouldn't tell me over the phone.

senses.definition: louder than something
examples: I couldn't hear what he said over the noise of the traffic.

senses.definition: in addition to something
examples: There are other factors over and above those we have discussed.

senses.definition: including all the things or people that are involved in a particular situation; general
examples: There will be winners in each of three age groups, and one overall winner.
the overall effect/picture/impression
When she finished painting, she stepped back to admire the overall effect.
The coach is happy with the overall performance of the team this season.
The overall quality of the articles is good.
The overall cost of my trip is £171.40.
a steady fall in overall crime rates
an overall result/impact/aim/strategy
the person with overall responsibility for the project
an overall improvement in standards of living (= affecting everyone)
The overall result is impressive.

senses.definition: including everything or everyone; in total
examples: The company will invest $1.6m overall in new equipment.
Last season he came second overall in the South African championships.
She kept the lead and won overall.

senses.definition: generally; when you consider everything
examples: Overall, this is a very useful book.
I think overall we're doing a good job.
Students were satisfied overall with what they had achieved.

senses.definition: to succeed in dealing with or controlling a problem that has been preventing you from achieving something
examples: She overcame injury to win the Olympic gold medal.
The two parties managed to overcome their differences on the issue.
He finally managed to overcome his fear of flying
He overcame a strong temptation to run away.

senses.definition: to defeat somebody
examples: In the final game Sweden easily overcame France.

senses.definition: to affect somebody very strongly
examples: Her parents were overcome with grief at the funeral.
Overcome by curiosity, the boy looked through the window.
The dead woman had been overcome by smoke.

senses.definition: to fail to see or notice something
examples: He seems to have overlooked one important fact.
In my hurry to finish the exam I had overlooked part of one of the questions.

senses.definition: to see something wrong or bad but decide to ignore it
examples: We could not afford to overlook such a serious offence.
He’s so friendly people are prepared to overlook his faults.
I was prepared to overlook her mistakes this time.

senses.definition: if a building, etc. overlooks a place, you can see that place from the building
examples: a restaurant overlooking the lake
Our back yard is overlooked by several houses.

senses.definition: to not consider somebody for a job or position, even though they might be suitable
examples: She's been overlooked for promotion several times.

senses.definition: too; very
examples: I'm not overly fond of pasta.
We think you are being overly optimistic.

senses.definition: during or for the night
examples: We stayed overnight in London after the theatre.

senses.definition: suddenly or quickly
examples: Don't expect it to improve overnight.
She became famous overnight.

senses.definition: connected with foreign countries, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean
examples: The firm is expanding into overseas markets.
overseas development/trade
overseas visitors/students
This was her first overseas trip as prime minister.

senses.definition: to or in a foreign country, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean
examples: to live/work/go overseas
The product is sold both at home and overseas.
He was working overseas for an oil company.

senses.definition: to watch somebody/something and make sure that a job or an activity is done correctly
examples: United Nations observers oversaw the elections.

senses.definition: if something overturns, or if somebody overturns it, it turns into a position in which the top of it is where the bottom of it normally is or on its side
examples: The car skidded and overturned.
He stood up quickly, overturning his chair.

senses.definition: to officially decide that a legal decision, etc. is not correct, and to make it no longer legally recognized
examples: to overturn a decision/conviction/verdict
His sentence was overturned by the appeal court.
He hopes that councillors will overturn the decision.

senses.definition: to show that a belief or an idea is not correct
examples: She completely overturned my preconceptions about film stars.
This argument seems convincing, but is easily overturned.

senses.definition: to have such a strong emotional effect on somebody that it is difficult for them to resist or know how to react
examples: She was overwhelmed by feelings of guilt.
The beauty of the landscape overwhelmed me.

senses.definition: to defeat somebody completely
examples: The army was overwhelmed by the rebels.
He totally overwhelmed the Spaniard, 6–2, 6–3,6–2, in the final.

senses.definition: to be so bad or so great that a person cannot deal with it; to give too much of a thing to a person
examples: We were overwhelmed by requests for information.

senses.definition: to cover somebody/something completely
examples: The wave broke through the wall and overwhelmed the town.

senses.definition: very great or very strong; so powerful that you cannot resist it or decide how to react
examples: The evidence against him was overwhelming.
The overwhelming majority of those present were in favour of the plan.
an overwhelming sense of loss
She had the almost overwhelming desire to tell him the truth.
You may find it somewhat overwhelming at first.
There was overwhelming support for our policies.

senses.definition: to have to pay somebody for something that you have already received or return money that you have borrowed
examples: She still owes her father £3 000.
I'm still owed three days' leave.
He owes more money than he can afford to pay.
Exactly how much do we owe?
She still owes £3 000 to her father.
The country owes billions of dollars to foreign creditors.
Most hospitals owe money to pharmaceutical companies for medicines and equipment.
How much do I owe you for the groceries?

senses.definition: to feel that you ought to do something for somebody or give them something, especially because they have done something for you
examples: I owe a debt of gratitude to all my family.
You owe it to your staff to be honest with them.
I owe a duty to my family.
You owe me a favour!
Thanks for sticking up for me—I owe you one (= I owe you a favour).
I think you owe us an explanation.
I think we're owed an apology.

senses.definition: to exist or be successful because of the help or influence of somebody/something
examples: He owes his success to hard work.
The play owes much to French tragedy.
I owe everything to him.
The town owes its existence to the minerals that lie below the mountains.
I owe him everything.
I knew that I owed the surgeon my life.

senses.definition: to have to obey and support somebody who is in a position of authority or power
examples: All serfs owed allegiance to a lord.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that something belongs to or is connected with somebody
examples: It was her own idea.
I saw it with my own eyes (= I didn't hear about it from somebody else).
Is the car your own?
Your day off is your own (= you can spend it as you wish).
He desperately wants to live his own life (= be independent and do whatever he wants).
Our children are grown up and have children of their own.
For reasons of his own (= particular reasons that perhaps only he knew about), he refused to join the club.
The accident happened through no fault of her own.
He wants to come into the business on his own terms.
I need a room of my own.
I have my very own room at last.
their own personal/unique/private beliefs
He was determined to go his own way.
I was almost completely in my own little world.
Most people want to live in their own homes as they age.

senses.definition: done or produced by and for yourself
examples: She makes all her own clothes.
He has to cook his own meals.

senses.definition: to have the opportunity to show how good or useful you are or something is
examples: When the traffic's this bad, a bicycle really comes into its own.

senses.definition: bad people often seem to have good luck
to do something to somebody in return for harm they have done to you; to get revenge
examples: I'll get my own back on him one day, I swear!

senses.definition: to have your own opinion and make your own decisions without being influenced by other people
examples: She has a mind of her own and isn't afraid to say what she thinks.
My computer seems to have a mind of its own!

senses.definition: to remain in a strong position when somebody is attacking you, competing with you, etc.
examples: Business isn't good but we're managing to hold our own.
She can hold her own against anybody in an argument.
The patient is holding her own although she is still very sick.

senses.definition: because of your personal qualifications or efforts, not because of your connection with somebody else
examples: She sings with a rock band, but she's also a jazz musician in her own right.

senses.definition: to like talking a lot or too much, usually without wanting to listen to other people
examples: She’s much too fond of the sound of her own voice.

senses.definition: alone; without anyone else
examples: I'm all on my own today.
She lives on her own.

senses.definition: without help
examples: He did it on his own.

senses.definition: to have something that belongs to you, especially because you have bought it
examples: Do you own your house or do you rent it?
I don't own anything of any value.
Most of the apartments are privately owned.
an American-owned company
Does anyone own this coat? It was left in a classroom.
Don’t tell me what to do—you don’t own me!
His father was a farmer who owned land and property.
He owns and operates an eco-tourism company.
Hospitals became publicly owned companies.

senses.definition: to manage and take responsibility for something
examples: The successful candidate will be responsible for owning and achieving sales targets.

senses.definition: to be completely successful, especially in a competition; to completely defeat somebody
examples: She owned the stage, performing a medley of hit songs.
He didn't just finish the course, he completely owned it.
Yeah right, she totally owned you, man!

senses.definition: to admit that something is true
examples: He owned to a feeling of guilt.
She owned (that) she had been present.

senses.definition: to behave in a very confident way that annoys other people, for example by telling them what to do
examples: She was acting as if she owned the place.

senses.definition: a person who owns something
examples: a dog/pet/gun owner
a business/property/store owner
The painting has been returned to its rightful owner.
He's now the proud owner of a cottage in Wales.
Would the owner of the black Mercedes parked in front of the gates please report to reception.
They were using the land with the permission of the owner.

senses.definition: the fact of owning something
examples: a growth in home ownership
Ownership of the land is currently being disputed.
to be in joint/private/public ownership
The restaurant is under new ownership.

senses.definition: a chemical element. Oxygen is a gas that is present in air and water and is necessary for people, animals and plants to live.
examples: The patient didn't seem to be getting enough oxygen.
